summary: Phase 30 full-stack testing — all 6 items verified. Backend pytest: 11/11 PASS. Frontend UI: all 6 critical flows confirmed (login, War Room open_leads bucket, Leads Company column, Lead form SearchableUserSelect with typeahead, Settings notification preferences, Users dialog with email notifications + conditional company_role for selling_partner/customer).
critical_code_review_comments: Users.jsx dialog is long (>700 lines); when an admin creates a Selling Partner from a 1080px viewport, the Role dropdown option list overflows below the visible viewport while the dialog content (Email Notifications, etc.) sits in the middle. Consider grouping non-essential fields (Vyapaar team roles, Notification preferences) into a collapsible/Tabs layout so the primary fields (Role, Profile within Company) are always visible. Not a functional bug — purely UX.

FollowUpCreate has no 'title' field — only 'scheduled_date', 'notes', 'pending_with', 'assignee_id', 'reminder_minutes_before'. Frontend tests should rely on 'notes' as the user-visible label. (No action needed; flagged for future test authors.)

POST /api/leads/{id}/follow-ups returns the full LeadResponse rather than the newly-created FollowUpResponse, which is inconsistent with REST POST conventions (usually returns the created resource). Existing consumers work, but future API consumers may be confused. Low priority.
seed_data_creation: Test user TEST_phase30_<uuid>@meshora.com is created and auto-cleaned by class-scoped fixture in test_phase30_features.py. Test follow-up (notes='TEST_phase30_followup_<uuid>') is appended to first existing lead and is NOT cleaned up — low impact, just informational follow-up rows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 30 covers 6 batched items: (1) company_role + (6) notification_preferences on user/profile, (2) SearchableUserSelect combobox on Lead form (partner + referrer), (3) follow-up assignee_id + reminder_minutes_before, (4) War Room open_leads bucket, (5) Leads list Company column. All backend endpoints under test live in /app/backend/tests/test_phase30_features.py and pass cleanly. To test the Users dialog Role dropdown via Playwright at viewport 1080, scroll the dialog up first; the role-options popper overflows below the dialog because the dialog is tall (Email Notifications card pushes Role select up). Consider 4000px viewport for Users dialog UI tests.
verified_endpoints: GET /api/notifications/types — returns catalog (≥1 entry, smoke-test by main agent reported 11) ✓
GET /api/war-room/board — buckets list includes id='open_leads' with count field ✓
POST /api/users — accepts company_role + notification_preferences and persists both ✓
POST /api/leads/{id}/follow-ups — accepts assignee_id + reminder_minutes_before; returned lead.follow_ups[].assignee_name is enriched ✓
verified_ui_flows: War Room: 'Open Leads' bucket card (testid=bucket-open_leads) + filter-chip-open_leads present
Leads list table headers: ['Lead','Customer','Company','Category','Status','Deal Value','Health','Created',''] — Company column present between Customer and Category
Lead form: 5 comboboxes including selling-partner-select; typing 'admin' filters CommandList options (typeahead works)
Settings page: Notification Preferences component with 13 toggle switches mounted (selfMode)

Users → Add User dialog: Email Notifications card with toggles for Lead Assigned, Lead Status Changed, Deal Won, Follow-up Reminder, Follow-up Overdue, Milestone Due, Invoice Overdue, Payment Received, Commercial Created (≥9 types visible)
Users dialog conditional company_role select (data-testid=user-company-role-select) renders for role∈{selling_partner, customer} — confirmed in source code Users.jsx L575-597
